title: ChronoEHR-Agent: A Time-Aware EHR Benchmark for Chronic-Disease Readmission Prediction
subtitle: English Methods/Results brief generated from completed local benchmark outputs
boundary_statement: This document summarizes a retrospective EHR data analysis workflow. It is not a medical question-answering system and does not provide diagnosis or treatment recommendations.

abstract.background: EHR prediction studies are sensitive to prediction time, follow-up windows, and leakage from variables that are only known after the intended prediction point.
abstract.objective: To summarize a local time-aware benchmark for 30-day readmission prediction across chronic-disease cohorts and to document prediction-time feature availability, leakage audit, and traditional baseline modeling.
abstract.methods: The benchmark uses MIMIC-IV v3.1 cohorts for diabetes, chronic kidney disease, heart failure, and hypertension. Models are evaluated at admission, within the first 24 hours, and before discharge using patient-level splits and traditional machine-learning baselines.
abstract.conclusion: The current results should be interpreted as a research-method benchmark and local analysis tool, not as a deployable clinical model.

methods.data_source: We used locally stored MIMIC-IV v3.1 data for retrospective structured EHR analyses.
methods.cohort_design: Four chronic-disease cohorts were identified using diagnosis-code based cohort definitions. Each index admission was linked to subsequent admissions to define all-cause 30-day readmission.
methods.prediction_times: ChronoEHR-Agent explicitly separates admission-time, first-24-hour, and discharge-time prediction settings. Each feature set is constrained to variables available at or before the corresponding prediction time.
methods.leakage_audit: The workflow audits outcome labels, outcome proxies, post-discharge follow-up variables, patient-level train-test overlap, and high-risk variables whose availability changes across prediction times.
methods.models: Baseline models include logistic regression, Random Forest, histogram gradient boosting, and calibrated variants when dependencies are available. Metrics include AUROC, AUPRC, Brier score, threshold summaries, calibration summaries, and decision-curve net benefit.

limitations: The benchmark is retrospective and depends on local MIMIC-IV preprocessing choices.

The current English brief is generated as reviewed Markdown and a review DOCX draft; the main manuscript DOCX preset remains scaffold-only until broader language-aware body-text templates are implemented.
Readmission is currently summarized as all-cause 30-day readmission, with sensitivity analyses for alternative definitions.
External datasets such as eICU and CHARLS are tracked but remain data-pending in the current local workspace.
